query: Has Russia announced any extension of its gasoline (motor gasoline/benzine) export ban beyond July 31, 2026? Look for any cabinet resolution, decree, or official statement from late June 2026 or early July 2026 reported by TASS, Reuters, or government.ru. Also describe the recent status of Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian refineries and domestic gasoline shortages as of late June 2026, and historically how many days before each deadline past gasoline export ban extensions were formally announced.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Bottom line: As of July 1, 2026, NO formal extension of Russia's gasoline export ban beyond July 31, 2026 has been announced.** The ban (in force April 1–July 31, 2026, via a government decree signed April 2, 2026, government.ru #58316, reported by TASS) legally remains set to expire July 31.

**Critical distinction — what WAS announced in late June/early July (easily confused with a ban extension, but is NOT):**
- **June 30:** The EEC Council extended zero *import* duties on fuel to mid-2027 (an import facilitation, the opposite of an export ban).
- **June 26–28:** Putin extended oil *price-cap* countermeasures to Dec 31, 2027 (unrelated).
- A diesel export ban was under discussion but deemed unnecessary by the Energy Ministry; the aviation kerosene ban runs to Nov 30, 2026.
- On June 28, Putin merely mentioned the "possibility" of temporary gasoline/kerosene/diesel export bans — intent signaled, no formal extension enacted.

**Refinery/shortage context (late June 2026):** Worst fuel crisis since 2009. ~30% of refining capacity offline; 8 of 10 largest refineries hit; Moscow refinery struck twice (June 16 & 18). Sales restrictions in 55 of 83 regions. Putin publicly admitted the shortage June 28–29. Reserves ~1.7 mn tons (−4% y/y).

**Historical timing precedent:** Russia formalizes these extensions right at the deadline. The end-2025 extension was verbally signaled by Novak on Sept 25, 2025 and formally decreed Sept 30, 2025 (gov.ru #56377) — ~1 day before the period's end. This pattern suggests any extension beyond July 31, 2026 would most likely be decreed in the final days of July 2026.

Full structured findings (key_facts, sources, data_points) have been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: Ban on gasoline exports from Russia extended to producers of petroleum products
sources.snippet: The Russian government has extended gasoline export restrictions effective until July 31, 2026, to direct producers of petroleum products. The corresponding decree has been signed.

sources.title: Russia Bans Producers From Exporting Gasoline Until July 31
sources.snippet: A new decree (government.ru/news/58316) extends the ban to producers, with exemptions only under intergovernmental agreements.

sources.title: Путин провел совещание по обеспечению топливом внутреннего рынка
sources.snippet: Government extended the export ban on gasoline and diesel until July 31, 2026; FAS/Energy Ministry propose cutting exchange minimum from 15% to 10% July 1–Sept 30.

sources.title: Россия продлит нулевую пошлину на экспорт бензина
sources.snippet: Government reinstated gasoline export restrictions on direct producers until July 31, 2026, in April 2026; does not apply to EAEU countries.

sources.title: Совет ЕЭК продлил на один год действие нулевых пошлин на ввоз отдельных видов топлива в ЕАЭС
sources.snippet: On June 30, 2026 the EEC Council extended zero import duties on gasoline, diesel, aviation, marine fuel for one year until mid-2027.

sources.title: Россия обратилась в ЕАЭС для продления нулевой пошлины на бензин
sources.snippet: Novak: Russia submitted application to EEC to extend zero export/import duty on gasoline; decision expected next week.

sources.title: Правительство рассматривает введение полного запрета на экспорт дизтоплива
sources.snippet: Novak: govt considering a several-month diesel export ban; gasoline ban for all companies in effect until July 31, 2026.

sources.title: Путин продлил срок действия ответных мер на потолок цены на российскую нефть
sources.snippet: Putin extended retaliatory price-cap export ban to Dec 31, 2027 (was to expire June 30, 2026).

sources.title: Тотальный запрет отложили, Россия продолжает отгружать бензин в Армению
sources.snippet: Full gasoline export ban April 1–July 31, 2026; aviation kerosene ban until November 30, 2026; total ban under intergovernmental agreements postponed.

sources.title: Russia Extends Gasoline Export Ban, Restricts Diesel Shipments
sources.snippet: Government said Tuesday (Sept 30, 2025) a new decree (government.ru/news/56377) prolongs gasoline export ban until Dec 31, applying to all exporters including producers.

sources.title: Russia Moves to Extend Gasoline Export Ban as Shortages Persist
sources.snippet: Novak said Thursday (Sept 25, 2025) govt plans to extend gasoline export ban through end of year, without specifying when the official announcement would come.

sources.title: Selbst in Moskau kein Benzin: Putin sieht Probleme für die Erdölnation Russland
sources.snippet: June gasoline output 15% lower y/y; Q2 refinery throughput fell 11% to ~4.7 mn b/d; ~30% of refining capacity out of operation (Carnegie).

sources.title: La Russie vit sa pire pénurie de carburant depuis 2009
sources.snippet: Worst fuel shortage since 2009; 55 of 83 regions restricted; 20+ strikes since Jan 2026; refining at lowest since 2009, 20%+ capacity offline.

sources.title: Comment l'Ukraine a créé une pénurie de carburant en Russie
sources.snippet: Putin acknowledged a 'certain deficit' June 28; 8 of 10 largest refineries hit; Moscow refinery hit June 16 & 18; 55 of 83 regions restricted by June 24.

sources.title: Russia imposes complete ban on export of gasoline and jet fuel
sources.snippet: June 30, 2026: gasoline/kerosene export ban to stabilize domestic market; reserves ~1.7 mn tons; no reported extension to EAEU countries.

sources.title: Что происходит с бензином в Москве 1 июля 2026 года
sources.snippet: July 1, 2026: govt has extended fuel export ban until July 31, 2026; AI-95 71.46–86 rubles/liter; purchase limits 20–30 liters.

key_facts: As of July 1, 2026, NO formal extension of Russia's motor gasoline export ban beyond July 31, 2026 has been announced; the ban legally expires July 31, 2026.
The current gasoline export ban runs April 1–July 31, 2026, established by a government decree signed April 2, 2026 (government.ru #58316, reported by TASS).
On June 30, 2026 the EEC Council extended ZERO IMPORT duties on gasoline/diesel/jet/marine fuel for one year to mid-2027 — an import measure, not an export-ban extension.
Putin signed a decree (June 26–28, 2026) extending oil price-cap countermeasures to Dec 31, 2027 — unrelated to the domestic gasoline export ban.
A full diesel export ban was under discussion (June 26/29 meetings) but the Energy Ministry deemed it unnecessary as of June 28; aviation kerosene ban runs to Nov 30, 2026.
On June 28, 2026 Putin indicated the 'possibility' of temporary export bans on gasoline/kerosene/diesel but enacted no formal extension of the gasoline ban's end date.
Ukraine conducted 20+ strikes on Russian oil infrastructure in 2026, hitting ~8 of the 10 largest refineries; ~30% of refining capacity is offline.
The Moscow (Kapotnya/Gazprom Neft) refinery, supplying ~40% of Moscow's gasoline, was struck twice on June 16 and June 18, 2026 and may be offline for months.
Fuel-sales restrictions were in place in at least 55 of Russia's 83 federal regions by late June 2026; 20–50 liter purchase caps common; Crimea/Sevastopol suspended retail sales.
Putin publicly admitted the fuel shortage on June 28–29, 2026 (first acknowledgment), calling it 'not critical' and 'difficult but controlled'; gasoline reserves ~1.7 mn tons (−4% y/y).
Historical precedent: the end-2025 gasoline ban extension was verbally signaled by Novak Sept 25, 2025 and formally decreed Sept 30, 2025 (gov.ru #56377) — roughly 1 day before period end.
Russia typically formalizes gasoline export-ban extensions within a few days of (often right at) the expiry deadline via a government resolution, preceded by a Novak verbal signal a few days earlier.

Share of Russian refining capacity offline

Ukrainian strikes on Russian oil infrastructure in 2026
